• Solid understanding of ETL/ELT methodologies, data warehousing principles,

and modern data engineering best practices.

• Experience designing and implementing data models to support analytics,

reporting, and operational use cases.

• Experience supporting or working with BI tools such as Sisense (or similar

platforms).

• Experience with CI/CD pipelines and version control practices (e.g., GitLab,

Jenkins, or equivalent).

• Experience working in fast-paced product environments with an emphasis on

delivery, maintainability, and minimizing technical debt.

• Strong communication skills with the ability to collaborate across technical and

non-technical stakeholders

BONUS QUALIFICATIONS

• Experience building lightweight data applications or internal tools using any of

the following frameworks such as Streamlit, Dash, Flask, Gradio, Shiny, or

Node.js.

• Ability to navigate ambiguity, prioritize effectively, and adapt to changing

business needs.

• Prior experience in financial services or regulated environments is a plus

source_jobs.title: Technical Publications Manager
source_jobs.description:
About the Role

You'll lead MeridianLink's technical documentation strategy and team, working closely with product, engineering, and support to ensure our customers have clear, comprehensive guides for every aspect of our lending platform. As a strong writer and thoughtful mentor, you'll set the direction for documentation—implementing and managing our authoring tools and infrastructure, defining content structure, standards, and processes—while developing your team's capabilities. You'll contribute hands-on to our most complex guides while creating space for your writers to grow and take ownership of their work. This role values both strategic thinking and collaborative leadership, with the opportunity to shape how technical communications function across the organization.

 

Key Responsibilities

 - Contribute hands-on to documentation while managing and mentoring writers; model quality standards through your own work

 - Design and implement documentation content structure and taxonomy across MeridianLink product suite; ensure consistency and discoverability

 - Build and maintain content authoring infrastructure (tool implementation, template management, publishing workflows)

 - Partner with support and CSM organizations to define a comprehensive plan for customer-facing content that incorporates technical documentation, FAQs, KBs, and training materials.

 - Manage technical writing team: hiring, mentoring, workload assignment, performance management

 - Authoring: Write substantial portions of administrator and user guides; mentor writers on complex/high-stakes content

 - Define documentation process and standards; establish style guide, review cadence, and quality gates

 - Partner with product and engineering on documentation scope, requirements, and release timing

 - Oversee documentation release cycle aligned with product launches

 - Analyze user feedback and support tickets to identify content gaps and prioritize improvements

Required Qualifications

 - 7+ years in technical writing; 3+ years managing writers at a SaaS company

 - Hands-on experience authoring large, complex administrator and user guides (single products or product families)

 - Hands-on implementation experience with enterprise authoring tools (MadCap Flare, Paligo, or equivalent) as well as knowledge bases; ability to set up projects, templates, and publishing workflows

 - Experience designing documentation structure and information architecture for multiple products or a large product suite

 - Demonstrated ability to move teams from ad-hoc to systematic approaches (process definition, taxonomy, tooling)

 - Experience using AI tools to generate content drafts and automate processes

Preferred Qualifications

 - Consumer or mortgage software lending experience

 - Exposure to API/developer documentation

source_jobs.title: Implementation Analyst
source_jobs.description:
The Implementation Analyst plays a critical role in delivering seamless implementations and high-impact managed services for both new and existing customers. This role owns the end-to-end lifecycle of client deployments across MeridianLink’s software solutions driving successful launches, ensuring adoption, and creating long-term value.

Serving as a trusted advisor, the Implementation Analyst partners closely with clients to manage expectations, meet key milestones, and ensure a smooth transition from implementation through ongoing use. Acting as a central point of coordination, the role collaborates cross-functionally to resolve challenges, optimize outcomes, and elevate the overall customer experience.

The Implementation Analyst brings a deeper level of expertise—educating clients on advanced platform capabilities, identifying opportunities to enhance utilization, and translating customer feedback into actionable insights for the Product organization to help shape future innovation.

Expected Duties:

 - Implementation Analysts will have a thorough understanding of MeridianLink’s licensed software and its capabilities to educate and provide best practice recommendations to clients

 - The role will be responsible for innovation and leadership in developing and implementing initiatives that support client goals and objectives

 - Expected to be responsible for client onboarding activities including core connectivity and third-party integrations while facilitating interactions between clients and other MeridianLink departments

 - Implementation Analyst will manage internal and external stakeholder communications and expectations through the deployment cycle with a focus on driving project resources toward project completion while providing the highest level of proactive and responsive client service in response to and in anticipation of client questions and requests

 - Expected to adhere to project schedules and effectively ensure all project resources meet expected timelines and organize tasks to maximize productivity and utilization

 - The role will proactively identify issues and facilitate sensible resolutions before they become escalation items

Qualifications: Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities

The role will support seasoned peers and management on difficult to complex aspects of the position.

 - Career-development professional within the subject area

 - Requires moderate skill set and proficiency in the subject area

 - Ability to conduct work assignments of increasing complexity under moderate supervision with some latitude for independent judgment

 - Bachelor’s degree and 2-4 years of relevant experience

 - Strong proficiency in working with Microsoft Systems

 - Teamwork attitude, flexibility, and ability to manage different responsibilities/priorities at the same time

source_jobs.title: Technical Recruiting Coordinator and Sourcer
source_jobs.description:
We are seeking a highly organized, detail-oriented, and proactive Technical Recruiting Coordinator & Sourcer to support our busy Talent Acquisition team. This is a contract role with anticipation to last through the end of this year. This role sits at the intersection of multi-channel sourcing, operational excellence, and strategic partnership management—and is accountable for delivering exceptional candidate experiences while building strong relationships with both internal stakeholders and candidates.

You understand what it takes to source great talent across multiple disciplines and experience levels, coordinate complex interview logistics at scale, and maintain meticulous documentation that keeps the recruiting engine running smoothly. If you're energized by balancing technical sourcing, interview coordination, agency management, and operational precision—and you thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative environment—this role offers meaningful impact on our ability to attract exceptional talent across MeridianLink.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Sourcing & Multi-Level Pipeline Development

 - Source and engage passive and active talent across channels such as LinkedIn Recruiter, resume databases, talent communities, and referral networks for a wide variety of roles and experience levels across the organization.

 - Build and maintain robust, healthy candidate pipelines spanning multiple disciplines: technical roles (software engineering, AI/ML, data science, platform, infrastructure, QA), product & design roles (product management, UX/UI design, product operations), business & operations roles (sales, customer success, finance, people operations, business development), and entry-level through executive positions.

 - Develop role-specific outreach strategies and draft thoughtful candidate communications that reflect MeridianLink's people-first, purpose-driven culture and resonate with candidates across different industries, backgrounds, and career stages.

 - Partner closely with recruiters and hiring managers to understand target profiles, refine sourcing strategies, calibrate on candidate quality, and maintain momentum in pipeline development.

 - Monitor sourcing metrics and adjust approach based on what's working, continuously improving quality of candidate submissions.

Interview Coordination & Scheduling Excellence

 - Schedule high-volume interviews across multiple time zones, hiring teams, departments, and interview stages with precision, professionalism, and responsiveness.

 - Coordinate all interview types—hiring manager screens, technical interviews, panel interviews, case study sessions, and executive conversations—managing complex scheduling logistics for multiple stakeholders.

 - Execute flawless interview logistics: maintain accurate calendar holds, reserve meeting spaces, send timely confirmations, manage reschedules, provide clear instructions to candidates, update interviewers, and handle all related communications with meticulous attention to detail.

 - Serve as the warm, responsive, professional point of contact for candidates and hiring teams throughout the interview process—ensuring positive experiences that reflect well on MeridianLink.

 - Track interview completion and follow-up items to keep recruiting momentum moving forward.

Third-Party Agency Management & Strategic Partnership

 - Build and nurture collaborative, productive relationships with external 3rd party recruiting agencies and contingency partners that align with our hiring needs and values.

 - Serve as the primary point of contact and liaison for agency partners—clarifying role requirements, communicating timeline expectations, providing regular feedback on submissions, and establishing clear performance expectations.

 - Evaluate agency candidate submissions for quality, fit, and alignment with role specifications and company culture standards.

 - Provide constructive, timely feedback to agencies on candidate rejections—including specif

notes: MeridianLink is a FinTech company with a strong focus on AI and lending technology. The company has a mix of technical and non-technical roles, but the majority of the postings are for technical positions.
